dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 for an overdose at apartments on 12th.
Ma'am, I'm Alex, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 tonight?
I did some drugs and now I feel like I'm going to die.
Can you tell me your name?
My name is Lisa.
Are you aware of where you are and what the time is right now?
I'm in my apartment in [CITY]. Not sure what the exact time is, but I called probably after 8 PM.
What month and year is it today, if you know?
Yeah, it's September 2020-maybe the 16th or 17th? I'm not 100% sure.
And where are you located, specifically?
Unit 12 at these apartments on 12th Street, I think. The address is redacted, but you already knew that, right?
What time do you believe it is right now?
Around 8 or 9 PM, I think. My phone's on its last call and not working now.
Just for clarity, why did you call 911 tonight?
Ma'am, I need to briefly assess your airway. Can I look at your mouth and throat?
Airway is patent and self-maintained. No signs of compromise.
Is it okay if I listen to your breathing now?
Respiratory rate is 16 per minute. Breath sounds are clear and equal bilaterally.
Pulse is 94, strong and regular. Blood pressure is 136/76.
Oxygen saturation is 100%.
What medications are you currently on? Or have you taken anything else recently?

I forgot to take my usual meds yesterday. So I did a line of Cocaine tonight, 4 mg Ketamine, some Cymbalta, and some Ativan too, maybe three or so pills total, I don't really know the amount exactly now.
What are the medications you usually take, if you remember?
If it's possible, can you rate your chest pain on a scale of 1 to 10?
It's about a 3. It's not hurting like it's going to tear me open, but it's definitely a presence in my chest.
Does anything help make the pain better at all?
It's better when I sit upright. I tried laying down earlier and it felt worse, so I've stayed up mostly since.
Is the pain staying in one spot or moving anywhere else?
It's right in my chest, nowhere else. It feels consistent and doesn't seem to go anywhere or change too much since it started maybe 30 minutes ago or a little less.
Pupils are equal, round, and reactive to light, bilaterally.
When did the chest pain begin, after taking the drugs?

Right after I did the drugs. It kicked in pretty fast, like 5, 10 minutes maybe after I took them all here tonight. It wasn't instant, but it was quick for me to notice it was there and start to register what it might mean-panicky stuff, bad things, I think my heart was in danger or something like that.
HEENT area is clear, with no visible trauma, deflections, swelling, or drainage at this time.
4-lead ECG being placed now. Make sure you're seated comfortably.
Results show sinus arrhythmia and baseline sinus rhythm at this time.
Heart rate is currently 94 and within normal range.
Breathing is regular with 16 breaths per minute.
Blood pressure is 136/76, stable for now.
Oxygen saturation is at 100% using pulse oximetry.
Current glucose is 147 and stable.
ECG findings confirm sinus arrhythmia with a baseline rhythm.
I need verbal consent to give 324 mg of aspirin. Any known allergies?
I'm allergic to Lamictal.
No conflict. Aspirin is being administered now.
Placing a 12-lead ECG now. Consent is assumed for transport protocol unless declined.
12-lead obtained. Findings show borderline but not concerning ST depression and minimal wave irregularities at this time.
[COMPANY_NAME] has been alerted and is accepting patients already.
Trying to place a 20-gauge IV for normal saline KVO. Pretty straightforward arm access.
IV successfully placed. Normal saline running at a low rate now, flow appears normal and clear.
Rechecking heart rate now-it is 111, elevated from before-need to adjust any interventions accordingly.
Respiratory rate is 11, slightly slower than the first assessment. Blood pressure is 117/74, open and maintained well. Oxygen saturation 100% again, stable.
Second 12-lead ECG is in process. Showing sinus tachycardia and nonspecific ST-T wave irregularities, still no acute cardiac involvement detected.
Is the chest pain still a 3 out of 10?
Yes, it's still 3. Doesn't feel worse, doesn't feel better-just as it was.
Could we confirm what substances were used again and your symptoms?
Cocaine, Ketamine, Cymbalta, and some Ativan. Same story.
Notes updated: stimulant use and possible cardiac involvement, allergy to Lamictal confirmed.
Abdomen is soft and not tender to palpation. No rigidity, guarding, or masses.
Back is free from any trauma or issues. Completion of visual inspection shows no signs of bruising, marks, or wounds on the back or spine.
Pelvis is stable and non-tender. No pain with pressure, buckling, instability, or signs of injury.
Trachea is midline and patent. No signs of jugular venous distension observed-everything looks consistent with normal baseline signs.
No injuries or deformities seen on extremities. PMS is intact with all four limbs-motor, sensory and potentially gross coordination maintained.
Preparing to move you to the stretcher. Are you able to help with positioning?
Yeah, I can help.
Assistance on the way.
Patient secured with five point harness. One rail up and Semi-Fowler supported for transport stability.
Upon arrival, care was passed off to the ED nurse in bed 20. No issues encountered during transport or on arrival.
324 mg of aspirin was given for possible cardiac pain after identified rhythm changes and tachycardia.
12-lead ECGs were performed. IV was started with normal saline per order-everything clean so far.
No signs of respiratory distress. Oxygen remains at 100%. Patient appears stable for now.
Patient transported using standard 4-person sheet draw method to ED bed.
EMR updated and all relevant data live-reported-everything should be up to date.
Monitor her condition and reach out if any new issues emerge.
That's all from our side here. Thanks for the support.
